title: landolphia comorensis
intro: Landolphia comorensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Comoros yellow bird's eye, native to the Comoros and Mayotte islands in the Indian Ocean. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Landolphia comorensis include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of Landolphia comorensis typically involve boiling the bark in water to create a decoction, which can be taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
